sections.a_description: Diseases that live in the person, not the project. The founder is the first point of failure — and the last one to admit it.

a_image.alt: Lone figure sitting at a desk in a dark room illuminated only by multiple monitor screens, founder isolation

sections.a_description: The diseases you carry as the person behind the project.

items.a_description: When the founder becomes the bottleneck. Every decision flows through one person, and the project can't breathe without them.

a_image.alt: Single hand gripping dozens of tangled puppet strings controlling many small marionettes at once, dramatic side lighting from above, dark background, shallow depth of field

items.a_description: The slow burn that turns passion into exhaustion. You're still shipping, but you stopped caring three months ago.

a_image.alt: Single burnt matchstick still faintly smoldering next to a row of fresh unlit matches, dark background, dramatic macro photography, warm ember glow against cold tones

items.a_description: You shipped it, people are using it, and you're still convinced someone will figure out you have no idea what you're doing.

a_image.alt: Cracked vintage mirror reflecting a distorted shadowy silhouette, fragments showing different uncertain expressions, dark moody room, dramatic side lighting, shallow depth of field

items.a_description: Researching, planning, reorganizing — anything but the one task that would actually move the project forward.

a_image.alt: Ornate hourglass with sand frozen mid-fall suspended in time, golden grains hanging motionless in the air, dark background, dramatic macro photography, warm side lighting

seo.title: Founder — Diseases That Live in the Person | Sickness Labs
seo.description: Founder syndrome, startup burnout, imposter syndrome, procrastination. The diseases that live in the person behind the project, not the project itself.
